title: Formula/Baby Food - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Formula/Baby Food 25 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BABY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides diapers and formula. Also offers access to used clothing, depending on availability. 1440 Birch Avenue Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Residents who self-identify as low income Hours: Monday 1:30pm-6pm, Tuesday-Friday 11am-3:30pm Email (541) 942-2176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides food boxes, clothing, shoes, bedding, personal and feminine hygiene products, diapers, baby food and formula, assistance in acquiring identification documents, gasoline and propane vouchers, and local bus tickets. Availability is subject to donations and eligibility. 1035 SE 2nd Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Food and clothing: By appointment only All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 928-4460 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GROWING TOGETHER FAMILY COMMUNITY CENTER EMERGENCY DIAPER CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency diapers, baby formula, and baby wipes. Clients are eligible to visits once per week and each child is eligible for one pack of diapers or one pack of pull-ups. 310 SW H Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-5 Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-noon Email (541) 864-0776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA AMISTAD PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 30 S Gun Club Road Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 838-0689 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER COLONIA LIBERTAD PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 2921 Saddle Club Street SE #1009 Salem, OR 97317 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 315-2290 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER NORTHGATE CENTER PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support such as community cafes, the Casa Infantil Project, Strengthening Families Parenting Classes, advocacy, diapers, baby formula, and other baby items. 3850 Portland Road NE Suite 130 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday noon-6pm Email (503) 363-1895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER CLOTHING AND BABY SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides maternity clothing and supplies for pregnant women and nursing mothers. Also provides baby clothing, baby blankets, sleepers, toys and children's books, as well as emergency supplies of diapers, formula, and baby food. Strollers, infant bathtubs, car seats, and pack and plays available dependent on availability. Parents are encouraged to become a client (which is free) and allows access to more services. Diapers and formula are available for emergency needs only and not provided on an ongoing basis. Mother & Child is not a clothing closet; it is a program which has supplies to assist families but also focuses on education and support. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Pregnant or parents/primary caregivers of a child aged birth to 5 years old. No eligibility requirements based on income. The client must be able to come to the Mother and Child Education Center to receive services. Diapers, clothing, and other goods cannot be mailed or delivered to clients. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm, Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS MONMOUTH RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with the following services: comprehensive information and referral, food boxes, a proof of homelessness document for a free Oregon identification card, prescription expense assistance, hygiene items, diapers, formula, transportation passes, and laundry passes. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Transportation passes: Once per month for medical appointments, job interviews, or other appointments supported by documentation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME ROSEBURG BLESSING BAGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers formula, baby clothing, baby furniture, and equipment. 445 NE Chestnut Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon Email (541) 464-2740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE COORDINATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about local child care, preschool, and kindergarten options. Supplies basic resources like clothing, formula, diapers, and wipes. Offers referrals and application assistance to other resources as needed. Hosts ongoing parenting classes, early learning workshops, and an annual early learning fair. 21920 SW Sherwood Boulevard Sherwood School District Office Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who live within the Sherwood School District Boundaries (do not have to have a child in school) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-3pm Email (503) 825-5087 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh food boxes including meat, vegetables, dairy items, bread, baby food, and formula. Pet food is regularly available in the pantry. Limited monthly Senior Home Deliveries provided. People are welcome to receive food boxes two times per month. Also provides TriMet bus fares. The Community Garden is available to low-income families to raise their own vegetables. Water, greenhouse space, and gardening classes are provided. Gardeners give back by giving 10% of their harvest to the pantry. 17805 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Food pantry and community garden: Low-income households Formula: Age 2 and under Monthly Senior Home Deliveries: Homebound seniors age 62 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides children’s clothing (sizes preemie through teens), maternity clothing, baby supplies (diapers, wipes, pull-ups, formula, and car seats), and toys, while supplies last. Also accepts donations 5415 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Ages 0-17 for clothing; expectant parents Hours: Clothing: By appointment only Donations: Tuesday-Friday 10am-3pm Email (503) 777-1443 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE CONTINGENT MY NEIGHBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects goods and services from community members to families and youth impacted by foster care. 809 N Russell Street #203 Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: -Resource families and foster children/youth -Biological families and relative providers with an open ODHS Child Welfare case Hours: No standard hours Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAMBINOS OREGON BASIC NEEDS SUPPLIES BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BAMBINOS OREGON BAMBUS BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BABY RESOURCES BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BABY RESOURCES COMMUNITY SHARING PROGRAM BABY SERVICES FISH OF ALBANY INC BASIC NEEDS GROWING TOGETHER FAMILY COMMUNITY CENTER EMERGENCY DIAPER CLOSET MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER PARENTING SUPPORT MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER PARENTING SUPPORT MANO A MANO FAMILY CENTER PARENTING SUPPORT MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER CLOTHING AND BABY SUPPLIES POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS RESOURCE CENTER SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME ROSEBURG BLESSING BAGS SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE COORDINATOR SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS ST MARK'S LUTHERAN CHURCH BETHLEHEM CHILDREN’S CLOTHES CLOSET THE CONTINGENT MY NEIGHBOR
